title: Charred Smoked Malaysian Chicken Tagine
description: A diabetic-friendly, aerated Malaysian dish featuring charred chicken, smoky spices and a hint of sweetness.
instructions: In a small bowl, mix together smoked paprika, brown sugar, salt, and a pinch of black pepper.
Rub the spice mixture all over the chicken breast, making sure to coat it evenly.
Heat the coconut oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Sear the chicken breast for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until it develops a nice char.
Transfer the Dutch oven to the preheated oven and cook for 15-20 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through.
While the chicken is cooking, prepare the tagine sauce. In a blender or food processor, combine ginger, garlic, chicken broth, green chilies, and a pinch of salt.
Blend the sauce until it's smooth and well combined.
Once the chicken is done, remove it from the oven and let it rest for 5 minutes.
Add the cooked chicken to the tagine sauce and stir to coat.
Garnish with fresh cilantro and a squeeze of lime juice.
Serve the charred chicken tagine hot, over a bed of steamed vegetables or with some fluffy rice.
